items.text: NOTICE: Fire alarm testing will be conducted in Maple Residence Hall on Thursday, October 9, from 10:00 AM to noon. Alarms will sound intermittently throughout the morning. Residents must vacate rooms during each test. Replacement key fobs, if misplaced during evacuation, cost $15 at the front desk.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must residents do while the testing is underway?

options.A: Report any concerns to the residence coordinator
options.B: Silence all personal alarm devices promptly
options.C: Remain seated inside their own rooms
options.D: Leave their rooms during every test

questions.explanation: The notice states that residents must vacate rooms during each test, which the correct option restates as leaving their rooms during every test.

questions.stem: In this notice, the word "intermittently" is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: Alarms that sound intermittently go off and on at repeated, separate intervals rather than continuously, matching option B.

items.text: NOTICE: The drinking fountains on the fourth floor of Kessler Hall will be shut off for pipe replacement from Monday, June 2, through Wednesday, June 4. Normal water service resumes Thursday morning. Meanwhile, please use the fountains near the ground-floor lobby entrance.

questions.stem: According to the notice, how long will the fourth-floor fountains be unavailable?

options.A: For three days, Monday through Wednesday
options.B: For the entire first week of June
options.C: For four days ending Thursday morning
options.D: For two afternoons during the replacement

questions.explanation: The shutoff runs from June 2 through June 4 and service resumes Thursday, so the fountains are down for the three days Monday through Wednesday.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a fourth-floor resident who needs a drink on Tuesday, June 3?

options.A: They should wait until Thursday morning arrives
options.B: They should use fountains on a different floor
options.C: They should purchase bottled water each day
options.D: They should report the leak to the maintenance office

questions.explanation: June 3 falls within the shutoff, and the notice directs people to the ground-floor lobby fountains, so a fourth-floor resident must use fountains on another floor.

items.text: Hi Daniel, Your parking permit for the fall semester is ready. Please pick it up at the Transportation Office in Warren Hall by Friday, September 12. Bring your student ID and a copy of your vehicle registration. The office is open 8:30 AM to 4:00 PM. Thanks, Ms. Alvarez

questions.stem: According to the email, what should Daniel take with him to the office?

options.A: His identification card and vehicle registration copy
options.B: His completed payment and a parking application form
options.C: His class schedule and residence hall address
options.D: His driver's license and current insurance papers

questions.explanation: The email asks Daniel to bring his student ID and a copy of his vehicle registration, restated as an identification card and vehicle registration copy.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about when Daniel is able to collect the permit?

options.A: He must collect it before classes begin Monday
options.B: He must collect it after the office closes
options.C: He must collect it during posted business hours
options.D: He must collect it at the residence hall

questions.explanation: Because the office is open only from 8:30 AM to 4:00 PM, Daniel can collect the permit only during those posted business hours.

items.text: NOTICE: Because of bridge repairs on Oak Avenue, the Green Line shuttle will follow a detour from March 16 to March 20. Temporary stops will be added at Miller Library and the Student Union. The single-ride fare remains $2. Buses run every fifteen minutes on weekdays.

questions.stem: According to the notice, where will the shuttle add new stops?

options.A: Near the closed Oak Avenue bridge crossing
options.D: Near the library and union stops

questions.explanation: The notice says temporary stops will be added at Miller Library and the Student Union, restated as the library and union stops.

questions.stem: In this notice, the word "detour" is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: A detour caused by bridge repairs is a temporary alternate path around the closure, matching option C.
